title: Prometheus Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on the Pull Model, PromQL, and Metric types.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the architecture used by Prometheus to collect metrics?

options.text: A Push Model: Agents on the servers constantly push data to the central database.
A Pull Model: The Prometheus server periodically makes HTTP GET requests to a /metrics endpoint on the target servers.
A Streaming Model: Data is streamed continuously via Kafka.
A Batch Model: Log files are uploaded via FTP nightly.

questions.question: Which Prometheus metric type is most appropriate for tracking the total, cumulative number of 404 HTTP errors since the server started?

options.text: Gauge
Histogram
Summary
Counter

questions.question: Which Prometheus metric type is most appropriate for tracking the current available RAM on a server, which heavily fluctuates up and down?

options.text: Counter
Gauge
Histogram
Summary
A wrapper around standard SQL required to query PostgreSQL databases.
A proprietary language used exclusively for configuring Kubernetes YAML files.
Prometheus Query Language, heavily optimized for selecting and aggregating time-series data.
A JavaScript framework for building Dashboards.

questions.question: What is the primary role of the Prometheus Alertmanager component?

options.text: To restart crashed Docker containers automatically.
To scrape the /metrics endpoints faster during high CPU load.
To handle deduplication, grouping, and routing of fired alerts to actual receivers like Slack or PagerDuty.
To index raw application logs for full-text search.
